name: server-messages
description: Broadcast scheduled server announcements with sequential or weighted shuffle-bag rotation.

messages.description: Messages to broadcast. Empty arrays are allowed; when no messages are configured the cronjob exits quietly without sending or advancing rotation state.

text.description: Exact message text to broadcast. Supports {playerCount} and {serverName} placeholders. Unknown placeholders are left unchanged, and each cron execution logs a warning listing any unsupported placeholder keys so admins can spot typos before they linger in live broadcasts. Must contain at least one non-whitespace character.

weight.description: Relative weight used only when order is set to random. If order is sequential, this field is ignored and can usually stay at 1. Enter a whole number from 1 to 20; larger values are rejected during validation.

interval.description: Announcement schedule in five-field cron format (minute hour day-of-month month day-of-week). The expression is interpreted in the configured timeZone below, so you can usually enter your local wall-clock schedule directly instead of converting it to UTC by hand. Common examples: */15 * * * * = every 15 minutes, 0 * * * * = at the top of every hour, 30 18 * * 1-5 = 18:30 on weekdays in the selected time zone. Zero-padded values are accepted, so both 5 9 * * 1-5 and 05 09 * * 1-5 are valid.

order.description: Message rotation strategy. Sequential sends messages in order and wraps back to the start. Random puts each message into a bag as many times as its weight, shuffles that bag, then sends one entry at a time. Weights only matter in random mode; sequential mode ignores them. In practice, higher-weight messages appear more often, but every weighted slot is used before the bag is reshuffled.

timeZone.description: IANA time zone used when evaluating the interval schedule. Pick one of the supported presets so scheduled times follow the selected zone reliably across daylight-saving changes.

broadcast-messages.description: Poll once per minute and broadcast the next configured server message when the configured interval matches.
